title: Spung Tree, Ta Prohm, Angkor, Cambodia - art and archaeology
highlights: ](Tetrameles nudiflora) at Ta Prohm is doing its usual job of supporting the monument and destroying it at the same time. Its roots pull apart the temple's building stones, which then become supported mostly by the root system itself. When the tree eventually dies, both tree and building will come crashing  ...    | Philosophically speaking, we are watching the dynamic play between Vishnu (the Preserver) and Shiva (the Destroyer), who are ultimately one and the same; but for a little while, the balance still holds. |  ...    ---

title: THAILEX - Thailand Travel Encyclopedia
highlights: Spung Trees, are large deciduous trees, with the botanical designation Tetrameles nudiflora. It can grow to over 45 meters tall, its trunk is buttressed, and the bark is shiny grey. title: 19 Things to Know About Ta Prohm | Facts, History & Photos
highlights: 1908: The French colonial authorities begin a restoration project on Ta Prohm, led by the Ecole Francaise d’Extreme Orient (EFEO). They decide to leave Ta Prohm in a “natural state” as an example of how most of Angkor looked on its  ...    The Ta Prohm temple is iconic for the giant trees that intertwine with its ancient stone structures. Two species are most notable: the sponge tree, also known as the silk-cotton tree (tetrameles nudiflora), locally known as the “thitpok” tree.  ...    Ta Prohm translates from Khmer to English as “Ancestor Brahma.” “
